word: G1846 - ἐξορύσσω - exorýssō - ex-or-oos'-so - Break up

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐξορύσσω

- Transliteration: Exorusso

- Phonetic: ex-or-oos'-so

- Definition:

1. to dig out, to pluck out (the eyes)

2. to dig through

- Origin: from G1537 and G3736

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1537 and G3736; to dig out that is (by extension) to extract (an eye) remove (a roofing): - break up pluck out.
